text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST
18-MONTH POST-SURGICAL SURVEILLANCE

PATIENT: Denise Okafor
CLINICAL INDICATION: 50-year-old woman with history of left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, triple-negative subtype, Stage IIB (T2N1M0), germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant. Status post neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense AC and weekly paclitaxel, followed by left modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection and right prophylactic mastectomy with bilateral immediate reconstruction. Pathology demonstrated non-pCR with residual carcinoma (ypT1cN1a, RCB Class III). Received post-mastectomy radiation therapy to left chest wall and regional nodes (60 Gy total to chest wall) and adjuvant capecitabine x 8 cycles; pembrolizumab permanently discontinued for grade 3 immune-mediated hepatitis at cycle 14. Completed 1 year adjuvant olaparib (300 mg BID). Currently off active therapy. Surveillance imaging at 18 months post-surgery. Prior 12-month CT (18 months ago from current scan) showed no evidence of disease. Tissue expander exchange to permanent silicone implants performed in the interim.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of IV iohexol contrast material. Images were acquired during portal venous phase. Axial images were obtained from the lung apices to the pubic symphysis, with multiplanar reformats in coronal and sagittal planes. Slice thickness 1.25 mm. No prior CT from today's scanner for direct side-by-side comparison; most recent prior CT available (approximately 6 months prior, 12-month post-surgical surveillance scan) reviewed for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The central airways are patent. The lungs are well aerated. Scattered minimal subsegmental atelectasis at the right and left lung bases, of no clinical significance.

A NEW finding is noted: a smooth, well-circumscribed, non-calcified soft tissue nodule in the right lower lobe, posterior basal segment, measuring approximately 4 mm in greatest axial dimension. This nodule was NOT identified on the prior 12-month post-surgical surveillance CT. There is no associated spiculation, pleural tethering, or satellite nodularity. No endobronchial lesion. No cavitation. The left lung is clear. No additional pulmonary nodules are identified elsewhere in either lung.

Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pleural nodularity or thickening.

Mediastinum: The mediastinum maintains normal contours. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. Prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal, and paraesophageal nodal stations are unremarkable. No anterior mediastinal mass. Trachea is midline.

Heart and Pericardium: Cardiac size within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. The visualized coronary arteries show no significant calcification.

Chest Wall and Bilateral Breast Region: Bilateral silicone breast implants are in place following staged tissue expander exchange, with normal implant morphology and no evidence of rupture or periprosthetic fluid collection on the right. Left chest wall demonstrates expected post-mastectomy and post-radiation soft tissue changes with mild subcutaneous fibrosis and skin thickening, stable in appearance compared to the prior study. No discrete enhancing nodule or mass at the left chest wall to suggest locoregional recurrence. The left axilla demonstrates expected post-dissection surgical changes; no new axillary nodal mass. Right axilla is unremarkable. No supraclavicular, infraclavicular, or internal mammary nodal mass identified.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth hepatic contours. Hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal hypodense or hypervascular lesion to suggest metastatic deposit or primary hepatic pathology. No biliary dilatation. Portal vein is patent. Hepatic veins and IVC are unremarkable. Given the patient's recent history of pembrolizumab-associated grade 3 immune hepatitis (now resolved), the liver parenchymal enhancement pattern is normal; no residual heterogeneity.

Spleen: Normal in size and morphology. No splenic lesion.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances normally throughout. No ductal dilatation, peripancreatic stranding, or pancreatic mass.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are slender and unremarkable. No adrenal nodule or mass.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical enhancement and no hydronephrosis. No renal mass or calculus.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The visualized bowel loops are within normal limits. No bowel wall thickening, mesenteric infiltration, or free intraperitoneal fluid. Appendix is not clearly visualized; no periappendiceal inflammatory change.

Retroperitoneum and Abdominal Lymph Nodes: No enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes. No para-aortic or aortocaval lymphadenopathy. Aorta is normal in caliber.

Ascites: None.

PELVIS:

Uterus and Adnexa: The uterus is present and of normal morphology for a premenopausal woman. No adnexal mass or free fluid. Ovaries are visualized bilaterally, grossly unremarkable on CT (MRI would provide superior soft-tissue characterization if clinically indicated). Note is made that risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy has been discussed given the patient's germline BRCA2 status; this procedure has not yet been performed based on available surgical history.

Bladder: Adequately distended and unremarkable. No bladder wall irregularity.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No enlarged external iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymph nodes.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:

Osseous Structures: Visualized osseous structures including the thoracic and lumbar spine, ribs, sternum, pelvis, and proximal femora are without aggressive lytic or blastic lesion to suggest skeletal metastasis. No vertebral body height loss. No cortical destruction. Degenerative changes at the lower lumbar spine are mild and age-appropriate. Note: CT has limited sensitivity for early marrow-infiltrating disease; correlation with clinical symptoms and consideration of bone scintigraphy or MRI is appropriate if osseous symptoms develop.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. New 4 mm smooth, non-calcified pulmonary nodule, right lower lobe posterior basal segment, not present on the prior 12-month post-surgical CT. In a patient with a history of triple-negative breast carcinoma and germline BRCA2 mutation, this finding warrants attention; however, given the small size, smooth morphology, and lack of associated features of malignancy, a benign etiology — such as an intrapulmonary lymph node or post-inflammatory granuloma — is considered most likely. Short-interval follow-up CT chest is recommended to document stability (Fleischner Society criteria applicable for this nodule size in a patient with relevant oncologic history).

2. No evidence of locoregional recurrence at the left chest wall, axilla, or regional lymph node stations.

3. No hepatic, adrenal, or other intra-abdominal lesion to suggest visceral metastasis. Liver parenchyma appears normal following prior pembrolizumab-associated hepatitis.

4. No osseous lesion suspicious for skeletal metastasis on CT.

5. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal, hilar, abdominal, or pelvic lymph nodes.

6. Bilateral silicone breast implants intact with no periprosthetic complication identified.

Dictated and reviewed by: Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging
Electronic signature applied.
